title: Loft Orbital to test AI models on spacecraft for Earth observation
summary: Loft Orbital is collaborating with NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory to test AI models on spacecraft for enhanced Earth observation. The initiative aims to improve Earth science monitoring through artificial intelligence.

title: Satellogic partners with SynMax to build intelligence services around upcoming Merlin constellation
summary: Satellogic has partnered with SynMax to develop intelligence services leveraging the upcoming Merlin constellation. The collaboration aims to meet the demand from defense and intelligence agencies for persistent surveillance capabilities.

title: China appears to be developing 7-meter-diameter reusable rockets
summary: China is reportedly developing 7-meter-diameter reusable rockets, as indicated by a state-funded tender for tank tooling, stainless steel forging, and launch pad planning.

title: Report: U.S. needs framework for responding to hostile acts in space
summary: A report highlights the need for a U.S. framework to address hostile acts in space amid concerns over China's expanding counterspace capabilities. Experts at a Mitchell Institute workshop struggled to define the boundary between competition and conflict in space.

title: NASA and Boeing still uncertain about when Starliner will return to flight
summary: NASA and Boeing are still unsure about the return-to-flight timeline for the CST-100 Starliner, with a potential delay of up to a year. Safety advisers note progress but highlight ongoing issues.

title: Trump executive order directs NASA to plan quantum space applications
summary: The White House issued an executive order to accelerate U.S. quantum technology development, including space applications for navigation, sensing, and secure communications. NASA has been directed to plan for these quantum space applications.

title: Rocket Lab launches satellite for U.S. Space Force Victus Haze responsive space exercise
summary: Rocket Lab launched a satellite for the U.S. Space Force's Victus Haze responsive space exercise. The mission involved a Rocket Lab spacecraft paired with a True Anomaly vehicle to demonstrate rapid threat characterization and rendezvous operations.

title: Chinese spaceplane releases object into orbit, according to commercial space surveillance
summary: China's secretive spaceplane has released an object into orbit during its fourth mission, as reported by commercial space surveillance firm LeoLabs. The purpose of the object remains undisclosed.

title: America is about to cede Africa’s space industry to China, and nobody’s talking about it.
summary: The article warns that the U.S. may lose influence in Africa's space industry to China, emphasizing the need for American engagement in the sector. It suggests that the Trump administration's 'America First in Africa' policy should prioritize this area.

title: Astroscale raises funding to support growth strategy
summary: Astroscale, a satellite servicing company, has secured funding to shift from technology demonstrations to a regular series of missions. The capital infusion will support its growth strategy.

title: Ignite a child's imagination with the stars: Our expert-picked Astronaut Galaxy Star Projector is a Prime Day steal at just $25
summary: Astronaut Galaxy Star Projector is being marketed as an affordable way to inspire children about space exploration following the Artemis 2 mission. The product is highlighted as a Prime Day deal at $25.

title: SpaceX Dragon says goodbye to the ISS | Space photo of the day for June 23, 2026
summary: SpaceX's Dragon cargo spacecraft departed from the International Space Station (ISS), as captured in a striking space photo. The event marks another successful resupply mission for the orbiting laboratory.

title: Searching for alien life: New model could help scientists home in on habitable exoplanets
summary: A new exoplanet model helps scientists identify potentially habitable planets by assessing their ability to retain atmospheres over long periods. This could significantly narrow the search for alien life among rocky worlds beyond Earth.

title: This 'improbable' exoplanet system is so wonky because of a weird object within
summary: A newly discovered exoplanet system exhibits unusual orbital patterns due to an unidentified massive object, offering insights into planetary formation around eccentric celestial bodies.

title: This beginner-friendly travel telescope is the reason I turned into a skywatcher
summary: A beginner-friendly travel telescope is available for around $80 during Amazon Prime Day sales, encouraging stargazing enthusiasts. The affordable telescope is highlighted as a gateway to astronomy for new skywatchers.

title: SpaceX launches its 1st 'Starfall' reentry capsule in early morning Falcon 9 liftoff
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its first 'Starfall' reentry capsule aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, demonstrating the vehicle's space operation and safe return capabilities.

title: This ball of stars named Terzan 5 may be one of the Milky Way's original building blocks
summary: Astronomers have identified Terzan 5, a globular cluster, as a potential original building block of the Milky Way due to its unusual properties. The team's findings suggest it may hold clues to the galaxy's early formation.

title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope arrives in Florida ahead of SpaceX Falcon Heavy launch this summer
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for final preparations ahead of its SpaceX Falcon Heavy launch. The mission aims to join other major space telescopes like Hubble and Webb in orbit.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' is streaming now on MGM+, and you can watch for just $0.99 for a limited time
summary: Project Hail Mary is now available for streaming on MGM+ at a limited-time price of $0.99. The movie follows Ryland and Rocky on an epic journey to save the galaxy.

title: SpaceX to test upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX is preparing to launch a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle on Tuesday. This test marks a significant step in the development of the vehicle's reentry capabilities.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This follows a busy month of activities aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing its Terran R heavy-lift rocket program, with the second stage now transported to Stennis Space Center. The company aims for a debut launch in 2026.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is approaching a static fire test. The focus is on the next steps following Flight 12.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for China's internet constellation plans.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marked a significant milestone for Japan's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification of the first stage of its Nova rocket, verifying its structural integrity. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. These veteran astronauts will prepare for the upcoming crewed lunar landing.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities. These events aim to engage the industrial community and provide updates on ESA's planned activities.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its potential to revolutionize satellite servicing and space station operations. The technology aims to enhance automation and reduce costs in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This initiative supports European space science by involving research institutes in space missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on young researchers to test and develop materials for spacecraft to endure space conditions. This team ensures the success of ESA's missions by bringing innovative solutions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on resilient or advanced technology in the space industry, though specific details are not provided.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical testing phase for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This process involves exposing the parachute to extreme temperatures to ensure its reliability during re-entry.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space secured $7 million in funding and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit.

title: Ubotica Raises $11M for Satellite-Based AI Tech
summary: Ubotica, a Dublin-based space AI company, raised $11 million to advance its AI-powered intelligence platform for maritime security. The funding will accelerate the commercialization of its satellite-based technology.

title: A Faster Path to Orbit: How Hosted Payloads Are Changing Access to Space
summary: Hosted payloads are revolutionizing space access by offering a faster and more economical alternative to traditional methods. Companies no longer need to build everything from scratch, reducing time and investment requirements.

title: Exclusive: Northwood Unveils New Antenna, Expanded Network Capacity
summary: Northwood has introduced a new antenna to enhance its ground station network capacity, enabling more data downlinks across a broader range of frequencies. This development supports the company's rapid global expansion of its physical ground station footprint.

title: ElevationSpace Closes $40M Series B
summary: ElevationSpace, a Japanese satellite reentry startup, secured a $40M Series B funding round, bringing its total raised to $63.5M.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite, aiming for a 2027 flight. The company's quantum lab test marks a significant milestone in quantum communication technology.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of its Mission X-3, where its autonomous orbital vehicle Jackal achieved all test objectives and is now fully commissioned.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space has shifted its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly contribute to lunar surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans to launch a mission closer to Earth this month, followed by its first GEO mission in 2027.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace has raised $25 million in a Series B funding round to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Rocket Lab Launches Responsive Victus Haze Mission for Space Force
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched the responsive space mission Victus Haze for the U.S. Space Force from New Zealand. The mission is part of the Tactically Responsive Space (TacRS) program.

title: Building a Global Framework for D2D With Skylo’s Mindel De La Torre
summary: Skylo's Mindel De La Torre discusses the rapid deployment of direct-to-device (D2D) satellite connectivity in millions of phones, transitioning from a milestone tech demonstration to a mainstream feature.

title: EchoStar Swaps SATS Ticker for ECHO to Emphasize Business Shift
summary: EchoStar has changed its stock ticker from 'SATS' to 'ECHO' to reflect its transition into a global connectivity business. The change took effect on June 24.

title: Jio Evaluates a Sovereign LEO Constellation for India
summary: Jio Platforms, India's largest telecom operator, is considering launching a sovereign Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) constellation to provide satellite broadband services in India. The company is exploring a dual approach to enhance connectivity through its own constellation.

title: MDA Space to Acquire Blue Canyon from Raytheon to Reach US Defense Market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring spacecraft manufacturer Blue Canyon Technologies from Raytheon to enter the U.S. defense market. The deal aims to strengthen MDA Space's position in the American space industry.

title: Former SES Exec John-Paul Hemingway to Lead Vast’s Satellite Business
summary: Former SES executive John-Paul Hemingway has joined Vast to lead its new satellite manufacturing business as senior vice president of satellite business development and commercialization. Hemingway will oversee business development and commercialization efforts for Vast's satellite ventures.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract. The company secured the contract from the Department of Defense.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has partnered with Iceye to utilize next-generation satellite data for flood monitoring. This collaboration aims to enhance Norway's flood prediction and response capabilities.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall have announced plans to form a joint venture aimed at enhancing sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The collaboration was formalized through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on June.

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT's first investment in the space sector. The acquisition was announced on June 18 by Stockholm-based EQT.
